prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `92e50de0`.
codex_response:
The rule is: find the single rare-color 3x3 motif placed inside one lattice cell, then copy that exact motif into every cell whose row-block parity and column-block parity match the source cell, clipping naturally at partial border cells. The `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint was consistent with the official examples, so I used it only as a starting hypothesis and confirmed it against the puzzle; no `notes.md` or new DSL primitive was needed.

Validation passed end to end: `92e50de0` is discoverable, `python -m arc2.shared.verify --task 92e50de0` passed all 4/4 official examples, `python -m arc2.shared.build --task 92e50de0 --n-examples 1000` produced the artifacts, and `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task 92e50de0` passed 1000/1000 generated examples. I also visually checked `preview_01.png`; the outputs look on-family, including the checkerboard placement and clipped edge cases.
